content.claim: Hungary has the highest level of segregation of students from disadvantaged and advantaged socioeconomic groups into different schools, and in 2022 PISA showed the highest segregation of most/least disadvantaged students in the EU
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Segregation patterns reflect multiple causes (geography, housing, school choice, church exemptions, tracking); cannot isolate 6/8-year gimnázium contribution
Does not distinguish between segregation caused by extended tracks versus other systemic mechanisms
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
